revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[arz:هوكى جى]][[ca:Hōki-ji]][[en:Hokki-ji]][[es:Hōki-ji]][[eu:Hokki-ji]][[fa:هوکی-جی]][[fr:Hokki-ji]][[gan:法起寺]][[ja:法起寺]][[ko:호키지]][[ru:Хокки-дзи]][[sv:Hokki-ji]][[tr:Hokki-ji]][[uk:Монастир Хоккі]][[zh:法起寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Hokiji02ds1920.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shōtoku-shū|qid=Q11611002|1=Shōtoku-shū}}|prefecture={{ill|Ikaruga|qid=Q379233|1=Ikaruga, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.62277778|N|135.74611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Prince Yamashiro|qid=Q3137695|1=Prince Yamashiro}}|part_of={{ill|Buddhist Monuments in the Hōryū-ji Area|qid=Q1333799|1=Buddhist Monuments in the Hōryū-ji Area}}; {{ill|Historical Sites of Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q11611000|1=Historical sites of Prince Shōtoku}}; {{ill|Q22120745|qid=Q22120745|1=Yamato Northern 88 Sacred Sites}}; {{ill|seven great temples founded by Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q11610998|1=seven great temples founded by Prince Shōtoku}}}} {{Short description|Buddhist temple in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hokki-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|法起寺}} | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hokiji02ds1920.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright= | alt = | caption = | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1873 Okamoto, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|en|Ikaruga, Nara|qid=Q379233|lt=Ikaruga-chō|1=Ikaruga, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma-gun|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Shōtoku-shū|1=Prince Shōtoku}} | deity = [[Kannon|Jūichimen Kannon]] (Avalokiteśvara) | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = [http://www.horyuji.or.jp/hokiji.htm www.horyuji.or.jp/hokiji.htm] | founded_by = | year_completed = 638 }} [[File:Hokiji03ds1536.jpg|thumb|150px|The three-storied pagoda of Hokki-ji, a National Treasure]] {{Nihongo|'''Hokki-ji''' or '''Hōki-ji'''|法起寺||the ‘temple of the Arising Dharma’}}{{cite web|last=Schumacher|first=Mark|title=Shotoku Taishi |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/shotoku-taishi.html | quote = Couriously, there is a [[Shingon]] deity in Japan known as Hōki Bosatsu 法起菩薩 ...written with exactly the same as the characters of Hōkiji Temple, meaning “arising Dharma” or “awakening knowledge.”}} – formerly known as {{Nihongo|'''Okamoto-dera'''|岡本寺}} and {{Nihongo|'''Ikejiri-dera'''|池後寺}} – is a {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} temple in Okamoto, {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|en|Ikaruga, Nara|qid=Q379233|lt=Ikaruga|1=Ikaruga, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The temple's honorary ''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'' prefix is {{Nihongo|"'''Kōhonzan'''"|岡本山}}, although it is rarely used. The temple was constructed to honor [[:en:Avalokitesvara|Avalokitesvara]], and an 11-faced statue of the goddess is the primary object of worship in the temple. Hokki-ji is often considered to be one of the seven great temples founded by {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}, but in fact the temple was not completed until some decades after his death. In 1993, it was registered together with [[Hōryū-ji]] as an {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} under the name ''{{ill|Buddhist Monuments in the Hōryū-ji Area|en|Buddhist Monuments in the Hōryū-ji Area|qid=Q1333799|lt=Buddhist Monuments in the Hōryū-ji Area|1=Buddhist Monuments in the Hōryū-ji Area}}''. ==History== Hokki-ji is located in Ikaruga, a town that has long been a focal point of Japanese Buddhism, and the area contains numerous other old temples related to Prince Shotoku, such as {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Nara)|en|Hōrin-ji (Nara)|qid=Q655678|lt=Hōrin-ji|1=Hōrin-ji (Nara)}} and {{ill|Chūgū-ji|en|Chūgū-ji|qid=Q1091232|lt=Chūgū-ji|1=Chūgū-ji}}. Hokki-ji is located on a foothill to the northeast of Hōryū-ji Tō-in. It is said that the temple lies atop the ruins of {{Nihongo|Okamoto no Miya|岡本宮}} palace, wherein Prince Shōtoku had lectured on the {{ill|en:Lotus Sutra|en|en:Lotus Sutra|qid=Q861212|lt=en:Lotus Sutra|1=Lotus Sutra}}, and that according to the prince's last will and testament, his son, {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|qid=Q3137695|lt=Prince Yamashiro|1=Prince Yamashiro}} (Yamashiro no Ōe no ō) rebuilt the former palace as a temple. Excavation conducted around the temple grounds has revealed the remains of a building, the pillars of which were in direct contact with the earth (i.e. there was no foundation stone), confirming that another building had occupied the grounds prior to Hokki-ji. The temple is laid out such that the main hall and the pagoda are aligned along the east-west axis, similar to the layout of Hōryū-ji Sai-in. However, Hokki-ji's main hall is in the west, and the tower is in the east; the opposite of Hōryū-ji Sai-in. This kind of temple layout has come to be known as "Hokki-ji style". ==Cultural value== The only original 8th-century building remaining is the 24m-high three-storied pagoda, which is the oldest of its kind in Japan, and a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}. The lecture hall is a 1694 reconstruction, and Shōten-do hall is an 1863 reconstruction. The shrine contains a wooden 3.5m-tall 11-faced statue of [[:en:Avalokitesvara|Avalokitesvara]] that was constructed in the latter half of the 10th century. A copper image of a bodhisattva constructed in the latter half of the seventh century is currently entrusted to the {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|qid=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum|1=Nara National Museum}}. Both of these items have been designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. == Preservation == The three-story pagoda of Hokki-ji was repaired in 1262 and 1678. After the Meiji period, there have been two conservation projects, from 1898 to 1899 and from 1972 to 1975.{{Cite web |title=4. State of preservation/conservation |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/suisensyo/horyuji/MAINTEXT/4c2.html}} Between 1972 and 1975, restorers completely demolished the building and then put it back again. Modern restorers removed the decayed mortise and tenon structures from the columns and beams and a11ed new modules. They burned the date "shdwa 48" into these new modules for easier identification by later restorers.{{Cite book |last=Lothar Le11erose |title=Ten Thousand Things -- Module and Mass Production in Chinese Art |publisher=Princeton University Press |year=1998 |isbn=9780691009575 |location=Princeton, New Jersey |pages=121-127}} Fire prevention systems such as fire detection systems and water fire extinguishing systems have also been established. Records of the details of these works, including the results of investigations, plans and photographs were made into reports and published in 1975. == See also == *{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|qid=Q3245523|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)}} == References == {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Hokkiji}} *[http://www.horyuji.or.jp/en/ Hokiji homepage] *[http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara_e/area03/index.html Nara Prefectural Tourist Guide for Ikaruga] {{Coord|34|37|22|N|135|44|46|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Authority control}} [[en:Hokki-ji]] [[arz:هوكى جى]] [[ca:Hōki-ji]] {{Commons category|Hokkiji}} [[es:Hōki-ji]] [[eu:Hokki-ji]] [[fa:هوکی-جی]] [[fr:Hokki-ji]] [[gan:法起寺]] [[ja:法起寺]] [[ko:호키지]] [[ru:Хокки-дзи]] [[sv:Hokki-ji]] [[uk:Монастир Хоккі]] [[zh:法起寺]] {{wikidata link|Q1351209}} [[Category:638 establishments]] [[Category:7th-century Buddhist temples]] [[Category:7th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Prince Shōtoku]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 630s]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]]
